title: Collections, Taxonomy, Ordering, Navigation, Feeds, and Sitemaps · Runtime Lens: Pagination Orphans
summary: Navigation depends on filesystem order, tags differ by case, drafts leak into feeds, pagination duplicates pages, orphan content is invisible, and sitemap URLs disagree with canonical routes. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pagination orphans increment in reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Define page size, stable boundaries, first-page route, canonical behavior, empty pages, orphans, and changed insertion cases.
Build global, local, breadcrumb, previous-next, related, and multiple-way navigation from an acyclic visible route graph.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Pagination Orphans · Theory Pagination Orphans
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 8494 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-pagination-orphans — Define page size, stable boundaries, first-page route, canonical behavior, empty pages, orphans, and changed insertion cases.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-navigation-taxonomy-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-navigation-taxonomy-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_navigation_taxonomy_2(published: int = 13, navigable: int = 13, taxonomy_terms: int = 5, feed_entries: int = 13, sitemap_urls: int = 13) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if published < 1 or navigable < 0:
        return False, navigable, "publication-count"
    if navigable != published:
        return False, navigable, "orphan-route"
    if taxonomy_terms < 1:
        return False, taxonomy_terms, "taxonomy-shape"
    if feed_entries != published or sitemap_urls != published:
        return False, feed_entries, "derived-publication-drift"
    assert feed_entries == sitemap_urls == published
    return True, navigable, "reconciled-site-graph"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-pagination-orphans-gq7pii
# Competency: ssg-pagination-orphans.
# Case 7840: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_pagination_orphans_gq7pii(published: int = 13, navigable: int = 13, taxonomy_terms: int = 5, feed_entries: int = 13, sitemap_urls: int = 13) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_gq7pii = "gq7pii-iip7qg-7840"
    assert evidence_variant_gq7pii
    evidence_axes_gq7pii = (
        "readergq7pii",
        "source7840",
        "parseiip7qg",
        "routegq7pii7840",
        "artifact7840gq7pii",
        "recoveryiip7qg7840",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_gq7pii)) == 6
    if published < 1 or navigable < 0:
        return False, navigable, "publication-count"
    if navigable != published:
        return False, navigable, "orphan-route"
    if taxonomy_terms < 1:
        return False, taxonomy_terms, "taxonomy-shape"
    if feed_entries != published or sitemap_urls != published:
        return False, feed_entries, "derived-publication-drift"
    assert feed_entries == sitemap_urls == published
    return True, navigable, "reconciled-site-graph"

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A theory publishing team handles case 1827 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define page size, stable boundaries, first-page route, canonical behavior, empty pages, orphans, and changed insertion cases.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Pagination remains stable when new content is inserted at the beginning.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define page size, stable boundaries, first-page route, canonical behavior, empty pages, orphans, and changed insertion cases. Artifact under review: reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Pagination Orphans, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Pagination Orphans record says reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect pagination orphans evidence boundary for Pagination Orphans.

For inspect pagination orphans evidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Pagination Orphans for reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pagination Orphans model · Theory Pagination Orphans
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Pagination Orphans: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 9208 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define page size, stable boundaries, first-page route, canonical behavior, empty pages, orphans, and changed insertion cases.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pagination Orphans model · Theory Pagination Orphans.
steps.why: Retrieving Pagination Orphans through the concrete reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pagination Orphans model · Theory Pagination Orphans, keep Pagination Orphans connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define page size, stable boundaries, first-page route, canonical behavior, empty pages, orphans, and changed insertion cases.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pagination Orphans model · Theory Pagination Orphans, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Pagination Orphans precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pagination Orphans model · Theory Pagination Orphans, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 9208 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define page size, stable boundaries, first-page route, canonical behavior, empty pages, orphans, and changed insertion cases.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pagination Orphans model · Theory Pagination Orphans, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Pagination remains stable when new content is inserted at the beginning.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-pagination-orphans-gq7pii.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-pagination-orphans-gq7pii(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_pagination_orphans_gq7pii\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?published < 1 or navigable < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?navigable != published)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?taxonomy_terms < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?feed_entries != published or sitemap_urls != published)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
